## Security constraints

> ⚠️ These rules are locked at the organization level and cannot be overridden.

### No hardcoded secrets
Never hardcode API keys, tokens, or passwords. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
<!-- Source: Shon Paz (org) -->

### Input validation required
All external input must be validated with a schema (Zod, Joi, etc.).
<!-- Source: Shon Paz (org) -->

### No PII in logs
Never log emails, phone numbers, SSN, or other personal data.
<!-- Source: Shon Paz (org) -->

## Architecture standards

### Structured error responses
All API errors must return { error: { code, message } } format.
<!-- Source: Shon Paz (org) -->

### Use Python
Use Python as part of the standard tech stack.
<!-- Source: Frontend (team) -->

### Use FastAPI
Use FastAPI as part of the standard tech stack.
<!-- Source: Frontend (team) -->

### Use PostgreSQL
Use PostgreSQL as part of the standard tech stack.
<!-- Source: Frontend (team) -->

### Use Pytest
Use Pytest as part of the standard tech stack.
<!-- Source: Frontend (team) -->

### Use Kubernetes
Use Kubernetes as part of the standard tech stack.
<!-- Source: Frontend (team) -->

## Conventions

### Use UTC timestamps everywhere
Use UTC timestamps everywhere
<!-- Source: Shon Paz (org) -->

### Input validation required
Input validation required
<!-- Source: Shon Paz (org) -->

### Write unit tests for new code
Write unit tests for new code
<!-- Source: Frontend (team) -->

### Use conventional commits
Use conventional commits
<!-- Source: Frontend (team) -->

### No direct commits to main
No direct commits to main
<!-- Source: Frontend (team) -->
